prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans-type) gap of H2O; units eV
Level of theory: density-functional theory, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (tier “standard”)
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz; single-point only, no relaxation (DFT//exp-geom)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, treated with RKS)
Software/backend: PySCF 2.x via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 7 cycles; no engine warnings reported; density fitting approximation active by default; Koopmans gap is not a true excitation energy.
Live .out log: written during the run to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpu1wla05v/frontier_*.out (available for inspection).

Result
HOMO = –8.6037183 eV
LUMO =  +0.33695037 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV (Koopmans approximation)

No warnings were returned by the engine (warnings array empty). All integrity checks passed (integrity.trustworthy = true). The value above is reported exactly as produced by chemkit; provenance = computed.
